28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass

278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()

351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,

402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)

404	    ### V projection LoRA weights

408	    @staticmethod

421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):

423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors

430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431

433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection

1951	def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
1952	    self,
1953	    repo_id              : str,
1954	    tokenizer            = None,
1955	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1956	    first_conversion     : str = None,

1958	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
1959	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
1960	    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
1961	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
1962	    create_pr            : bool = False,
1963	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1964	    revision             : str = None,
1965	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",

1968	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,

1970	    """
1971	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1972	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1973	
1974	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1975	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1976	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1977	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1978	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1979	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1980	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1981	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1982	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1983	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1984	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1985	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1986	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1987	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1988	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1989	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1990	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",

1993	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",

1995	    if tokenizer is None:

2001	    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
2002	    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = False # We save ourselves
2003	    arguments["save_method"]   = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2004	    del arguments["self"]
2005	    del arguments["repo_id"]
2006	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
2007	    del arguments["first_conversion"]

2016	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
2017	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

235	def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
236	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],
255	        base_layer.bias,

167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)

182	        pass

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

235	def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
236	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],
255	        base_layer.bias,
256	    )

Excellent! Now let me compile all the findings into a comprehensive summary. Let me create a detailed report with all the information I've found:

## Summary of LoRA Adapter Structure in Unsloth Codebase

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here is a comprehensive report on how LoRA adapter weights are structured and accessed:

---

### **1. LoRA A and B Matrices Storage**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329933260-tye47x/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 214-231)

**Attribute Names and Access Pattern:**
- **lora_A**: Stored in `proj.lora_A[adapter]` where `adapter` is the active adapter name (default is "default")
- **lora_B**: Stored in `proj.lora_B[adapter]`
- **Weight tensors**: Accessed via `.weight` property on each (e.g., `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`, `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`)

**Tensor Shapes:**
- **lora_A shape**: `(r, input_dim)` - projection from input dimension down to rank `r`
- **lora_B shape**: `(output_dim, r)` - projection from rank `r` up to output dimension
- The full LoRA update: `output = X @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T * scaling`

**Code reference (lines 214-219):**
```python
lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
A = lora_A_linear.weight
B = lora_B_linear.weight
```

---

### **2. LoRA Rank (r) and Alpha Values Storage**

**Files with rank/alpha definitions:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329933260-tye47x/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2268-2271)

### **1. LoRA A and B Matrices Storage**

**Attribute Names and Access Pattern:**
- **lora_A**: Stored in `proj.lora_A[adapter]` where `adapter` is the active adapter name (default is "default")
- **lora_B**: Stored in `proj.lora_B[adapter]`
- **Weight tensors**: Accessed via `.weight` property on each (e.g., `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`, `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`)

**Tensor Shapes:**
- **lora_A shape**: `(r, input_dim)` - projection from input dimension down to rank `r`
- **lora_B shape**: `(output_dim, r)` - projection from rank `r` up to output dimension
- The full LoRA update: `output = X @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T * scaling`

Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report of my findings.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Unsloth Codebase Structure and Save/Export Functionality

### 1. **Overall Directory Layout**

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point, environment setup
├── _auto_install.py           # Auto-installation utilities
├── chat_templates.py          # Chat template management (129KB)
├── import_fixes.py            # Import-time fixes for transformers, PEFT, TRL
├── save.py                    # MAIN SAVING & EXPORT MODULE (2678 lines, 105KB)
├── tokenizer_utils.py         # Tokenizer utilities including GGUF fixes (41KB)
├── trainer.py                 # Custom trainer patches (8.5KB)
│
├── kernels/                   # Low-level optimized kernels
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── utils.py              # CRITICAL: LoRA parameter extraction
│   ├── fast_lora.py          # Fast LoRA forward/backward passes
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py  # Custom loss computation
│   ├── flex_attention.py
│   ├── geglu.py
│   ├── layernorm.py
│   ├── rope_embedding.py
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py
│   ├── swiglu.py
│   └── moe/                  # Mixture of Experts kernels
│
├── models/                    # Model loading and patching
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── loader.py             # FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, etc.
│   ├── llama.py              # Llama-specific optimizations (131KB)
│   ├── vision.py             # Vision model support (47KB)
│   ├── _utils.py             # Shared utilities (68KB)
│   ├── mapper.py             # Layer name mapping
│   ├── loader_utils.py
│   ├── mistral.py
│   ├── qwen2.py
│   ├── qwen3.py
│   ├── qwen3_moe.py
│   ├── granite.py
│   ├── cohere.py
│   ├── gemma.py
│   ├── gemma2.py
│   ├── falcon_h1.py
│   ├── rl.py                 # RL training patches
│   ├── rl_replacements.py
│   ├── dpo.py                # DPO training patches
│   └── llama4.py
│
├── registry/                 # Dynamic model registry
├── dataprep/                 # Data preparation utilities
└── utils/                    # General utilities
```

---

### 2. **Main Save/Export Functions Locations**

All save/export functionality is centralized in **/unsloth/save.py** (2678 lines)

**Core Public API Functions** (exposed via `patch_saving_functions`):
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main unified save function (line 205)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` - Save merged models (line 1321)
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` - Push merged to HF Hub (line 1364)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Save as GGUF format (line 1768)
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Push GGUF to HF Hub (line 1951)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_ggml()` - Save LoRA as GGML (line 2211)
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_ggml()` - Push GGML to HF Hub (line 2143)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` - TorchAO quantization (line 2502)
- `unsloth_generic_save()` - Generic save for non-Llama models (line 2356)
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - Generic merged save (line 2413)

---

### 3. **LoRA Adapter Data Structures**

LoRA adapters are accessed through PEFT library structures. Located in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-256):

**Key Extraction Functions:**
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Returns: (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
    """
    # Get base layer weights
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract LoRA matrices
    # - lora_A: shape (r, in_features) - projection to rank
    # - lora_B: shape (out_features, r) - projection back
    # - scaling: alpha/r scaling factor
    lora_A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight      # (r, in_features)
    lora_B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight      # (out_features, r)
    scaling = proj.scaling[adapter]           # scalar: alpha / r
```

**Data Structure:**
- **A matrix** (`lora_A`): Low-rank projection matrix
  - Shape: `(rank, in_features)`
  - Located: `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight`
  
- **B matrix** (`lora_B`): Reconstruction matrix
  - Shape: `(out_features, rank)`
  - Located: `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`
  
- **Scaling factor**: Alpha value divided by rank for LoRA
  - Located: `proj.scaling[adapter_name]`
  
- **Target modules**: Defined during LoRA initialization
  - Common: `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"]` (attention)
  - Or: MLP layers `["gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`

**Quantization state** (`quant_state`):
- For 4-bit models: Contains dequantization parameters
- Accessed via: `W.quant_state` on quantized weights

---

### 4. **GGUF Export Pipeline**

**Function:** `save_to_gguf()` at line 952

**Steps:**
1. **Merge LoRA**: Convert 4-bit + LoRA to 16-bit FP16
   - Uses `_merge_lora()` for each layer (line 159)
   - Formula: `W_merged = W_base + A.T @ (s * B.T)`
   
2. **Install llama.cpp** (non-blocking):
   - Clones: `https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp`
   - Builds with Make or CMake
   
3. **Convert to GGUF**: 
   - Calls: `python llama.cpp/convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
   - Output: `unsloth.{f16|bf16|q8_0}.gguf`
   
4. **Quantize GGUF**:
   - Calls: `llama-quantize` for each requested quantization method
   - Supports: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `q3_k_*`, etc.
   - Output: Multiple GGUF files at different quantization levels

**Key signature:**
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,  # "float16" or "bfloat16"
    is_sentencepiece: bool = False,
    model_directory: str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method = "fast_quantized",  # ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", ...]
    first_conversion: str = None,
    _run_installer = None,
):
```

**Quantization options** (ALLOWED_QUANTS dict at line 79):
- `not_quantized`: Full precision (f32/f16/bf16)
- `fast_quantized`: Q8_0 (default)
- `quantized`: Q4_K_M (recommended)
- Various Q-formats: q2_k, q3_k_*, q4_k_*, q5_k_*, q6_k
- Special: iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs

---

### 5. **Adapter Saving (LoRA)**

**Function:** `unsloth_save_model()` at line 205 with `save_method="lora"`

**For LoRA-only saving:**
- Delegates to PEFT's native `save_pretrained(selected_adapters=None)`
- Saves only adapter matrices: `adapter_config.json`, `adapter_model.bin`
- Base model weights not included

**Files produced:**
- `adapter_config.json`: LoRA configuration
  - Contains: `r`, `alpha`, `target_modules`, `lora_dropout`, etc.
- `adapter_model.bin`: A and B matrices in state_dict format

---

### 6. **Merged Model Saving**

**Function:** `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` or `"merged_4bit"`

**Process:**
1. **Memory management**: Calculate max RAM/VRAM usage
2. **Layer-by-layer merging**:
   - For each layer in `model.layers`:
     - Extract LoRA matrices via `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
     - Dequantize if 4-bit: `fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)`
     - Merge: `W.addmm_(A.T, B.T, alpha=scaling)`
     - Convert to target dtype (f16/bf16)
   
3. **Save state dict**:
   - If safe_serialization enabled: Use safetensors
   - Otherwise: PyTorch pickle with optimal protocol
   - Can shard across multiple files if needed

**Key function for merging:**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merges LoRA into base weights"""
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        # Dequantize 4-bit weights
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # W += (A.T @ (s * B.T))
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), 
                 B.t().to(torch.float32), 
                 alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

---

### 7. **How Saving Methods are Exposed/Registered**

**Registration occurs in:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` and `/unsloth/models/vision.py`

**Function:** `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2569 of save.py

**Process:**
1. Model is loaded via `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`
2. LoRA is applied via `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()`
3. `patch_saving_functions(model)` is called
4. Methods are attached via `types.MethodType()`:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # For language models:
    model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
    model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
    model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
    model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
    model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
    model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
    model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**Also patches:**
- `push_to_hub()` method with Unsloth wrapper
- Stores original as `original_push_to_hub`

---

### 8. **Main Entry Points and Usage**

**For standard saving:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    "my_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"  # or "lora", "merged_4bit"
)
```

**For GGUF export:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "my_model_gguf",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"  # or "fast_quantized", ["q4_k_m", "q8_0"], etc.
)
```

**For HF Hub:**
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged(
    "username/my_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)
```

---

### 9. **Critical Files Summary**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | 2678 | ALL save/export functions, GGUF conversion, HF Hub integration |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 793 | LoRA parameter extraction: `get_lora_parameters()`, `get_lora_parameters_bias()` |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 2902 | Llama-specific patching, calls `patch_saving_functions()` |
| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | 964 | Vision model patching, calls `patch_saving_functions()` |
| `/unsloth/__init__.py` | 300+ | Environment setup, module initialization |

---

### 10. **Key Constants and Configurations**

**Llama-specific layer names** (line 67-75 in save.py):
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)
```

**HF Hub metadata tagging:**
- All saves automatically tagged with `["unsloth"]` tag
- Commit message auto-modified to include "Trained with Unsloth"
- Model card template defined at line 1410

---

This comprehensive structure shows Unsloth's sophisticated approach to model saving with support for multiple export formats, hardware-aware memory management, and seamless HF Hub integration.

- `not_quantized`: Full precision (f32/f16/bf16)
- `fast_quantized`: Q8_0 (default)
- `quantized`: Q4_K_M (recommended)
- Various Q-formats: q2_k, q3_k_*, q4_k_*, q5_k_*, q6_k
- Special: iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs

---

### 5. **Adapter Saving (LoRA)**

**Function:** `unsloth_save_model()` at line 205 with `save_method="lora"`

**For LoRA-only saving:**
- Delegates to PEFT's native `save_pretrained(selected_adapters=None)`
- Saves only adapter matrices: `adapter_config.json`, `adapter_model.bin`
- Base model weights not included

**Files produced:**
- `adapter_config.json`: LoRA configuration
  - Contains: `r`, `alpha`, `target_modules`, `lora_dropout`, etc.
- `adapter_model.bin`: A and B matrices in state_dict format

---

### 6. **Merged Model Saving**

**Function:** `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` or `"merged_4bit"`

**Process:**
1. **Memory management**: Calculate max RAM/VRAM usage
2. **Layer-by-layer merging**:
   - For each layer in `model.layers`:
     - Extract LoRA matrices via `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
     - Dequantize if 4-bit: `fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)`
     - Merge: `W.addmm_(A.T, B.T, alpha=scaling)`
     - Convert to target dtype (f16/bf16)
   
3. **Save state dict**:
   - If safe_serialization enabled: Use safetensors
   - Otherwise: PyTorch pickle with optimal protocol
   - Can shard across multiple files if needed

**Key function for merging:**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merges LoRA into base weights"""
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        # Dequantize 4-bit weights
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # W += (A.T @ (s * B.T))
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), 
                 B.t().to(torch.float32), 
                 alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

---

### 7. **How Saving Methods are Exposed/Registered**

**Registration occurs in:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` and `/unsloth/models/vision.py`

**Function:** `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2569 of save.py

**Process:**
1. Model is loaded via `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`
2. LoRA is applied via `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()`
3. `patch_saving_functions(model)` is called
4. Methods are attached via `types.MethodType()`:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # For language models:
    model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
    model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
    model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
    model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
    model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
    model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
    model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(
        unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**Also patches:**
- `push_to_hub()` method with Unsloth wrapper
- Stores original as `original_push_to_hub`

---

### 8. **Main Entry Points and Usage**

**For standard saving:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    "my_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"  # or "lora", "merged_4bit"
)
```

**For GGUF export:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "my_model_gguf",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"  # or "fast_quantized", ["q4_k_m", "q8_0"], etc.
)
```

**For HF Hub:**
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged(
    "username/my_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)
```

---

|------|-------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | 2678 | ALL save/export functions, GGUF conversion, HF Hub integration |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 793 | LoRA parameter extraction: `get_lora_parameters()`, `get_lora_parameters_bias()` |

## COMPREHENSIVE GGUF EXPORT IMPLEMENTATION ANALYSIS

Based on my deep dive into the unsloth codebase, here's the complete architecture for GGUF export and related saving patterns that you can model your ONNX export after:

## CORE GGUF EXPORT FUNCTIONS

### 1. **`save_to_gguf()` - Main GGUF Conversion Engine**

**Location:** Lines 952-1318  
**Signature:**
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type           : str,
    model_dtype          : str,
    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized",  # Can be str or list
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    _run_installer = None,  # Non-blocking install of llama.cpp
) -> tuple[list[str], bool]:  # Returns (file_locations, full_precision_seen)
```

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Installs/locates llama.cpp tools (quantize, convert-hf-to-gguf.py)
- Converts HuggingFace model to GGUF format (float16/bfloat16)
- Applies quantization methods (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, etc.)
- Handles Sentencepiece tokenizer fixing
- Manages Kaggle environment limitations
- Returns list of output file locations and whether full precision exists

**Quantization Options:** Maps user-friendly names to actual formats:
- `"not_quantized"` → f16/bf16
- `"fast_quantized"` → q8_0
- `"quantized"` → q4_k_m
- Direct options: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q2_k, q3_k_l, q3_k_m, q3_k_s, q4_0, q4_1, q4_k_s, q5_0, q5_1, q5_k_s, q6_k, iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs, q3_k_xs

---

### 2. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Public GGUF Export Method**

**Location:** Lines 1768-1948  
**Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,  # Model instance (added via patch_saving_functions)
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,  # REQUIRED - raises ValueError if None
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
) -> None
```

**Workflow:**
1. Validates tokenizer requirement
2. Prepares arguments for `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
3. Fixes BOS token in tokenizer chat template if needed (`fix_tokenizer_bos_token()`)
4. Installs llama.cpp non-blocking (parallel with model merge):
   - Kaggle: blocking install with CUDA disabled
   - Other: git clone + make in background
5. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` to merge LoRA weights to 16bit
6. Extracts model dtype and type from config
7. Checks if model uses Sentencepiece tokenizer
8. Calls `save_to_gguf()` with merged model
9. Creates Ollama Modelfile if available in tokenizer
10. Optionally uploads to Hugging Face Hub

---

### 3. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Hub Upload Variant**

**Location:** Lines 1951-2125  
**Signature:**

```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,  # REQUIRED

    first_conversion     : str = None,
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,

    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
) -> None
```

**Same pattern as `save_pretrained_gguf()` but:**
- Takes `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`
- Sets `push_to_hub=False` internally (self-manages upload)
- Uploads all GGUF files to hub
- Creates model card automatically

## ADAPTER WEIGHT PROCESSING

### 4. **`_merge_lora()` - LoRA Merging**

**Location:** Lines 159-187  
**Signature:**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
```

**Process:**
1. Extracts LoRA parameters from layer using `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
2. If layer is quantized (Bnb_Linear4bit or Peft_Linear4bit):
   - Dequantizes weight matrix using `fast_dequantize()`
   - Converts to float32, transposes
   - Adds LoRA: `W += A.t() @ (s * B.t())`
   - Validates finiteness of merged weights
   - Converts back to original dtype, transposes
3. Returns weight tensor and optional bias tensor

---

### 5. **`unsloth_save_model()` - Core Saving Engine**

**Location:** Lines 204-700+ (extends to line 2273)  
**Decorator:** `@torch.inference_mode`  
**Signature:**
```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,

    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    # Push to hub params
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    # Unsloth-specific params
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
) -> tuple[str, Optional[str]]
```

**Key Features:**
- Validates save_method
- Prohibits `merged_4bit` unless forced with `merged_4bit_forced`
- Memory management for Kaggle/Colab environments
- Smart CPU/GPU RAM/Disk usage calculation
- Tokenizer padding side management (set to "left" for inference)
- Automatic tag addition (adds "unsloth" tag)
- Handles PEFT and non-PEFT models differently

**For merged_16bit (GGUF preparation):**
- Iterates through model layers
- Calls `_merge_lora()` for each layer weight
- Stores state_dict to GPU/RAM/Disk strategically
- Saves using safe_serialization or pickle

  - `model.save_pretrained_ggml` → `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally`

## CRITICAL DESIGN PATTERNS FOR ONNX EXPORT

Based on this architecture, here's how to model your ONNX export:

### Pattern 1: User-Facing Method
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,  # REQUIRED (validate!)
    opset_version: int = 17,  # Your parameter
    optimize: bool = True,
    **standard_hf_kwargs
):
    # 1. Validate tokenizer
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("...")
    
    # 2. Fix tokenizer BOS token
    fix_bos, old_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
    
    # 3. Prepare merged weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    arguments["push_to_hub"] = False
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit"
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["opset_version"]
    del arguments["optimize"]
    
    # 4. Call merge function
    merged_dir, username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    
    # 5. Call ONNX conversion
    onnx_files = save_to_onnx(
        model_type=self.config.model_type,
        model_dtype=dtype_from_config(self.config),
        model_directory=merged_dir,
        opset_version=opset_version,
        optimize=optimize,
    )
    
    # 6. Handle push_to_hub if needed
    if kwargs.get("push_to_hub"):
        for file_loc in onnx_files:
            upload_to_huggingface(...)
```

### Pattern 2: Core Conversion Function
```python
def save_to_onnx(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,
    model_directory: str,
    opset_version: int = 17,
    optimize: bool = True,
    **kwargs
) -> list[str]:
    # Similar structure to save_to_gguf():
    # 1. Validate parameters
    # 2. Check/install dependencies (onnxruntime, onnx-simplifier, etc.)
    # 3. Load merged model
    # 4. Convert to ONNX
    # 5. Optimize (quantize, simplify, etc.)
    # 6. Validate output
    # 7. Return file locations
```

### Pattern 3: Registration in patch_saving_functions
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model
)
model.push_to_hub_onnx = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_push_to_hub_onnx, model
)
```

---

## KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS FOR REFERENCE

**File Management:**
- Merged weights saved to `temporary_location` if RAM insufficient
- Uses `map_location="cpu"` with `mmap=True` for efficiency
- Automatic cleanup on Kaggle/Colab

**Memory Strategy:**
- Tracks GPU VRAM, system RAM, disk space separately
- Calculates max_vram based on `get_device_properties().total_memory`
- Safe_serialization is slower but uses more RAM (sharded)
- Fallback to pickle on low-CPU systems

**Error Handling:**
- Clear error messages with suggestions
- Kaggle-specific disk space warnings
- Validation of quantization methods against ALLOWED_QUANTS
- Finiteness checks on merged weights (prevents NaN/inf)

**Tags & Metadata:**
- Automatically adds "unsloth" tag to all saves
- Modifies commit messages to include "(Trained with Unsloth)"
- Creates model cards automatically
- Sets `tokenizer.padding_side = "left"` for inference

---

This architecture gives you everything you need to implement ONNX export following the exact same patterns, including argument structure, validation, memory management, registration, and Hugging Face integration.

3. Fixes BOS token in tokenizer chat template if needed (`fix_tokenizer_bos_token()`)
4. Installs llama.cpp non-blocking (parallel with model merge):
   - Kaggle: blocking install with CUDA disabled
   - Other: git clone + make in background
5. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` to merge LoRA weights to 16bit
6. Extracts model dtype and type from config
7. Checks if model uses Sentencepiece tokenizer
8. Calls `save_to_gguf()` with merged model
9. Creates Ollama Modelfile if available in tokenizer
10. Optionally uploads to Hugging Face Hub

---

### 3. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Hub Upload Variant**

**Location:** Lines 1951-2125  
**Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,  # REQUIRED
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
) -> None
```

**Same pattern as `save_pretrained_gguf()` but:**
- Takes `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`
- Sets `push_to_hub=False` internally (self-manages upload)
- Uploads all GGUF files to hub
- Creates model card automatically

---

## ADAPTER WEIGHT PROCESSING

### 4. **`_merge_lora()` - LoRA Merging**

**Location:** Lines 159-187  
**Signature:**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
```

**Process:**
1. Extracts LoRA parameters from layer using `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
2. If layer is quantized (Bnb_Linear4bit or Peft_Linear4bit):
   - Dequantizes weight matrix using `fast_dequantize()`
   - Converts to float32, transposes
   - Adds LoRA: `W += A.t() @ (s * B.t())`
   - Validates finiteness of merged weights
   - Converts back to original dtype, transposes
3. Returns weight tensor and optional bias tensor

---

### 5. **`unsloth_save_model()` - Core Saving Engine**

**Location:** Lines 204-700+ (extends to line 2273)  
**Decorator:** `@torch.inference_mode`  
**Signature:**
```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    # Push to hub params
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    # Unsloth-specific params
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
) -> tuple[str, Optional[str]]
```

**Key Features:**
- Validates save_method
- Prohibits `merged_4bit` unless forced with `merged_4bit_forced`
- Memory management for Kaggle/Colab environments
- Smart CPU/GPU RAM/Disk usage calculation
- Tokenizer padding side management (set to "left" for inference)
- Automatic tag addition (adds "unsloth" tag)
- Handles PEFT and non-PEFT models differently

**For merged_16bit (GGUF preparation):**
- Iterates through model layers
- Calls `_merge_lora()` for each layer weight
- Stores state_dict to GPU/RAM/Disk strategically
- Saves using safe_serialization or pickle

---

## REGISTRATION & PATCHING SYSTEM

### 6. **`patch_saving_functions()` - Method Registration**

**Location:** Lines 2569-2678  
**Signature:**
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision : bool = False) -> model:
```

**Behavior:**
- Wraps original `push_to_hub()` with unsloth-enhanced version
- Uses `inspect.signature()` to copy original signature
- Dynamically generates wrapped function via `exec()`
- Uses `types.MethodType()` to bind methods to model instance
- For non-vision models, adds these methods to model:
  - `model.push_to_hub_merged` → `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged`
  - `model.save_pretrained_merged` → `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged`
  - `model.push_to_hub_gguf` → `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf`
  - `model.save_pretrained_gguf` → `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf`
  - `model.save_pretrained_torchao` → `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao`
  - `model.push_to_hub_ggml` → `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`
  - `model.save_pretrained_ggml` → `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally`
- For vision models: registers only merged and gguf variants (no LoRA-specific)
- Recursively traverses nested model.model attributes

---

## HELPER FUNCTIONS

### 7. **`check_if_sentencepiece_model()`**
**Location:** Lines 116-134
```python
def check_if_sentencepiece_model(
    model, 
    temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"
) -> bool
```
Checks if model tokenizer is Sentencepiece by attempting to save and checking for `tokenizer.model` file.

### 8. **`fix_tokenizer_bos_token()`**
**Location:** Lines 1586-1615
```python
def fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer) -> tuple[bool, str]:
```
Returns (needs_fixing, original_chat_template). Removes BOS token from chat template if model auto-adds one to prevent duplication in GGUF.

### 9. **`create_ollama_modelfile()`**
**Location:** Lines 1618-1653
```python
def create_ollama_modelfile(tokenizer, gguf_location) -> Optional[str]:
```
Generates Ollama Modelfile from tokenizer's `_ollama_modelfile` attribute if present.

### 10. **`fix_sentencepiece_gguf()`**
**Location:** Imported from `unsloth.tokenizer_utils`
Fixes Sentencepiece tokenizer metadata before GGUF conversion.

### 11. **`dtype_from_config()`**
**Location:** Imported from `unsloth_zoo.hf_utils`
```python
def dtype_from_config(config) -> Union[str, torch.dtype]:
```
Extracts torch_dtype from model config.

---

## GENERIC SAVE VARIANTS (For Non-LLAMA Models)

### 12. **`unsloth_generic_save()`**
**Location:** Lines 2355-2410
```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_generic_save(model, tokenizer, save_directory, ...) -> None:
```
Calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` for non-standard model architectures.

### 13. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`**
**Location:** Lines 2413-2453
Method wrapper calling `unsloth_generic_save()`.

### 14. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`**
**Location:** Lines 2456-2499
Method wrapper calling `unsloth_generic_save()` with `push_to_hub=True`.

### 15. **`save_to_gguf_generic()`**
**Location:** Lines 2276-2352
For vision/non-LLM models. Converts to GGUF using `_convert_to_gguf()`.

---

## CONSTANTS & CONFIGURATION

**ALLOWED_QUANTS Dict** (Lines 79-107):
Maps 26 quantization method names to descriptions.

**LLAMA_WEIGHTS Tuple** (Lines 67-70):
Layer weight names to merge:
- `self_attn.q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`
- `mlp.gate_proj`, `up_proj`, `down_proj`

**LLAMA_LAYERNORMS Tuple** (Lines 71-75):
Normalization layers to save as-is.

**Environment Detection** (Lines 59-64):
- `IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT`
- `IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT`
- `KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"`

---

## CRITICAL DESIGN PATTERNS FOR ONNX EXPORT

Based on this architecture, here's how to model your ONNX export:

### Pattern 1: User-Facing Method
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,  # REQUIRED (validate!)
    opset_version: int = 17,  # Your parameter
    optimize: bool = True,
    **standard_hf_kwargs
):
    # 1. Validate tokenizer
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("...")
    
    # 2. Fix tokenizer BOS token
    fix_bos, old_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
    
    # 3. Prepare merged weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    arguments["push_to_hub"] = False
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit"
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["opset_version"]
    del arguments["optimize"]
    
    # 4. Call merge function
    merged_dir, username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    
    # 5. Call ONNX conversion
    onnx_files = save_to_onnx(
        model_type=self.config.model_type,
        model_dtype=dtype_from_config(self.config),
        model_directory=merged_dir,
        opset_version=opset_version,
        optimize=optimize,
    )
    
    # 6. Handle push_to_hub if needed
    if kwargs.get("push_to_hub"):
        for file_loc in onnx_files:
            upload_to_huggingface(...)
```

### Pattern 2: Core Conversion Function
```python
def save_to_onnx(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,
    model_directory: str,
    opset_version: int = 17,
    optimize: bool = True,
    **kwargs
) -> list[str]:
    # Similar structure to save_to_gguf():
    # 1. Validate parameters
    # 2. Check/install dependencies (onnxruntime, onnx-simplifier, etc.)
    # 3. Load merged model
    # 4. Convert to ONNX
    # 5. Optimize (quantize, simplify, etc.)
    # 6. Validate output
    # 7. Return file locations
```

### Pattern 3: Registration in patch_saving_functions
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model
)
model.push_to_hub_onnx = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_push_to_hub_onnx, model
)
```

---

## KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS FOR REFERENCE

**File Management:**
- Merged weights saved to `temporary_location` if RAM insufficient
- Uses `map_location="cpu"` with `mmap=True` for efficiency
- Automatic cleanup on Kaggle/Colab

**Memory Strategy:**
- Tracks GPU VRAM, system RAM, disk space separately
- Calculates max_vram based on `get_device_properties().total_memory`
- Safe_serialization is slower but uses more RAM (sharded)
- Fallback to pickle on low-CPU systems

**Error Handling:**
- Clear error messages with suggestions
- Kaggle-specific disk space warnings
- Validation of quantization methods against ALLOWED_QUANTS
- Finiteness checks on merged weights (prevents NaN/inf)

**Tags & Metadata:**
- Automatically adds "unsloth" tag to all saves
- Modifies commit messages to include "(Trained with Unsloth)"
- Creates model cards automatically
- Sets `tokenizer.padding_side = "left"` for inference

---

This architecture gives you everything you need to implement ONNX export following the exact same patterns, including argument structure, validation, memory management, registration, and Hugging Face integration.

from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames
KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"
del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",

    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass

    sentencepiece_model = False

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        try:
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        except:
            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
                # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(

    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):

            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])

            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
